name: Armenia

colors.symbolism: the Armenian Highland; the Armenians' continued struggle for survival, the maintenance of their Christian faith, and Armenian independence
the will of the Armenian people to live beneath peaceful skies
the creative talent and hard-working nature of the Armenian people

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red on top, blue in the middle, and orange (apricot) at the bottom.
history: Adopted on 24 August 1990 by the Supreme Council of the Armenian SSR, ahead of independence in 1991. The design revives the flag of the First Republic of Armenia (1918–1920), suppressed by Soviet annexation. The official symbolism was set out in the Constitution of Armenia (Article 14, 1995) and elaborated in the Law on the National Flag (2006).

features.symbolism: red for the Armenian Highland and the blood shed for the survival of the Armenian people, blue for the Armenian sky, orange for the country's creative talent and hard-working nature
